title: Fixtures, Golden Files, Properties, Fuzzing, HTML Validation, and Browser Tests · Language Clinic: Test Pyramid
summary: Tests snapshot everything, approve changed HTML blindly, omit malformed input, compare only strings, never mutate fixtures, and treat validator success as user-flow proof. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the test pyramid increment in layered parser-to-browser verification matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-ca…
objectives: Separate scanner, parser, renderer, route, asset, incremental, CLI, filesystem, validation, browser, accessibility, deployment, and recovery evidence.
Combine contextual encoding, URL validation, sanitization where required, strict templates, CSP, integrity, dependency review, and host headers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-testing-validation-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_testing_validation_3(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Test Pyramid evidence · Theory Test Pyramid
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-test-pyramid-1q9m8u" that uses case 8110 to combine mutation-sensitive parser, property, validation, and browser evidence. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Test Pyramid evidence · Theory Test Pyramid.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Test Pyramid into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Test Pyramid evidence · Theory Test Pyramid, keep Test Pyramid connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-test-pyramid-1q9m8u
# Competency: ssg-test-pyramid.
# Case 8110: a multilingual public-service handbook migration.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_test_pyramid_1q9m8u(fixture_cases: int = 48, detected_mutants: int = 46, required_mutants: int = 45, validator_errors: int = 0, browser_tasks_failed: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1q9m8u = "1q9m8u-u8m9q1-8110"
    assert evidence_variant_1q9m8u
    evidence_axes_1q9m8u = (
        "reader1q9m8u",
        "source8110",
        "parseu8m9q1",
        "route1q9m8u8110",
        "artifact81101q9m8u",
        "recoveryu8m9q18110",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1q9m8u)) == 6
    if fixture_cases < 10 or detected_mutants < 0:
        return False, detected_mutants, "test-shape"
    if detected_mutants < required_mutants:
        return False, detected_mutants, "mutation-gap"
    if validator_errors > 0:
        return False, validator_errors, "invalid-output"
    if browser_tasks_failed > 0:
        return False, browser_tasks_failed, "reader-flow-failure"
    assert detected_mutants >= required_mutants
    return True, detected_mutants, "layered-verification"

content.title: Evidence defense
